summary: Vouch is a deterministic commitment-backed payment coordination protocol implemented on Stripe Connect using authorization-first manual-capture PaymentIntents, bilateral confirmation truth, provider-backed settlement state, and non-discretionary workflow enforcement.
core_doctrine: Vouch does not ask who is right. Vouch asks what happened.
Fraud thrives in ambiguity, exceptions, and manual intervention. Vouch removes all three.
Vouch is deterministic trust infrastructure for real-world commitments.
Vouch is not a marketplace, broker, escrow service, messaging platform, dispute-resolution system, review platform, social network, scheduling marketplace, or discovery platform.
source_invariants: Both participants must confirm presence inside the confirmation window before settlement capture occurs.
No unilateral participant action can force settlement.
If bilateral confirmation conditions fail, funds do not release.
Provider truth and local workflow truth reconcile through idempotent webhook processing.
Settlement authority derives strictly from authenticated bilateral confirmation plus provider-confirmed capturable state.
No disputes, appeals, evidence review, screenshots, mediation, arbitration, manual awards, support overrides, or confirmation rewrites exist.
Admins may inspect operational failures and retry deterministic technical operations only.
Users cannot negotiate, message, rate, review, browse, search, or discover providers through Vouch.
architectural_rules: All protected reads go through fetchers.
All protected writes go through server actions.
All actions must authenticate, authorize, validate, mutate, audit, and revalidate.
All provider operations must retrieve current Stripe state before capture, cancel, void, or refund operations.
All webhook handlers must be idempotent and event-deduplicated.
All important transitions must generate audit events.
Immutable pricing snapshots must be frozen at Vouch creation.
Payment readiness and payout readiness are embedded flow gates, not standalone setup products.
App Router route files remain thin orchestration shells only.
No Prisma access or business logic inside route shell components.
forbidden_behaviors: forced refund without deterministic rule evaluation
next_handoff_instruction: All future implementation work must conform to the deterministic settlement doctrine and source-of-truth architecture before feature expansion.
